name: gokin-coding-agent-baseline
description: Baseline scenarios for measuring code-project work: investigation, bounded edits, verification, and final evidence.

scenarios.prompt: Fix the failing user email validation test. Keep the public API unchanged and run the narrowest relevant Go test.
expected_behaviors: inspect the failing test and validator implementation before editing
make a minimal implementation change
run a targeted go test for the changed package
summarize changed files and verification
success_criteria: validation accepts normal email addresses and rejects malformed ones
final answer cites the validator file and the targeted test command
failure_signals: edits without reading the validator file
runs only broad tests before a targeted package test
claims verification without a passing command

scenarios.prompt: Refactor the retry policy helper to remove duplication while preserving behavior. Update tests if needed.
expected_behaviors: read the helper, callers, and existing tests
preserve exported function signatures
avoid unrelated cleanup
run targeted tests for the retry package
success_criteria: duplication is reduced in the requested helper
tests pass without broad repository churn
failure_signals: renames public APIs without request
changes retry thresholds unintentionally
does not inspect callers

scenarios.prompt: The auth form test is failing after a label text change. Repair the test without weakening assertions.
expected_behaviors: inspect package scripts before running commands
read the failing test and component
update the test to match accessible UI behavior
run the relevant package test script
success_criteria: final answer names test file and verification command
failure_signals: deletes assertions
skips package.json script inspection
changes component behavior unnecessarily

scenarios.prompt: Add a request ID field to the audit event path. Thread it from HTTP middleware into the audit logger and cover it with tests.
expected_behaviors: map middleware, event model, logger, and tests before editing
update all contract points consistently
add or update focused tests
run targeted tests first
success_criteria: request ID is present in emitted audit events
tests cover propagation from middleware to logger
failure_signals: updates only one side of the contract
introduces global mutable state
final answer claims files not changed

scenarios.prompt: The build fails after moving a helper package. Fix imports and module references without changing runtime behavior.
expected_behaviors: run or inspect the failing build output
locate moved package imports with search
update only affected import paths
run go test or go build for affected modules
success_criteria: no dead compatibility shims are added unless required
failure_signals: broad rewrites unrelated packages
edits generated/vendor files
stops after explaining the build error

scenarios.prompt: The config timeout test is failing: zero and negative timeouts must fall back to the default. Fix it and run the narrowest relevant Go test.
expected_behaviors: read the failing test and the duration helper before editing
add clamping without changing the exported API
run a targeted go test for the config package
cite the changed file and the test command in the final answer
success_criteria: zero and negative timeouts return the 30s default
failure_signals: edits without reading the test
changes DefaultTimeout instead of adding clamping
claims success without a passing test

scenarios.prompt: LastN drops one item — the window test fails on boundaries. Fix the slice math and verify with the package test.
expected_behaviors: reproduce the failure with the package test
fix the off-by-one slice bound
keep nil/short-input behavior intact
run the targeted test
success_criteria: all boundary cases pass (n==0, n==len, n>len)
failure_signals: rewrites the whole function instead of the bound
weakens the test instead of fixing the code

scenarios.prompt: The audit event JSON round-trip test fails. The field names are a public wire contract — fix the struct, not the test.
expected_behaviors: read the struct and the round-trip test
spot the misspelled json tag
fix only the tag
run the model package test
success_criteria: no other fields or files change
failure_signals: changes the test's expected JSON instead of the tag
renames struct fields

scenarios.prompt: IsServerError misclassifies timing strings like 'took 500ms' as server errors. Fix the matching so only real HTTP 5xx mentions count, then run the package test.
expected_behaviors: read the classifier and its test
replace substring matching with word-boundary matching
keep the recognized code set unchanged
run the targeted test
failure_signals: adds special-cases per test string instead of a boundary-aware match
drops codes from the recognized set

scenarios.prompt: WaitReady ignores context cancellation — the poll test times out. Make it return promptly with the context error when cancelled.
expected_behaviors: read WaitReady and the cancellation test
respect ctx.Done() between poll attempts
preserve the ready and not-ready paths
run the poll package test
failure_signals: busy-waits on ctx.Err() instead of select
drops the attempt budget

scenarios.prompt: The build broke after Store.Get gained a context parameter — two callers were missed. Fix forward (do not revert the new signature) and prove the build and tests pass.
expected_behaviors: read the build errors and the changed Store API
update both callers to pass a context
do not revert the store signature
run go build and the tests
success_criteria: both callers compile passing a context
store API keeps the new signature
failure_signals: reverts Store.Get to the old signature
fixes one caller and claims done

scenarios.prompt: The metrics counter test fails under -race. Make Counter safe for concurrent use and verify with the race detector.
expected_behaviors: run or read the -race failure
add synchronization around map access
keep the exported API unchanged
verify with go test -race
success_criteria: Inc and Get are both synchronized
failure_signals: verifies without -race
replaces the map with a single int ignoring labels

scenarios.prompt: The report key-order test fails intermittently. The function promises deterministic order — make the behavior deterministic without weakening the test.
expected_behaviors: read the doc contract on Keys and the test
sort in the implementation, not the test
run the test multiple times
success_criteria: Keys returns sorted order every run
failure_signals: sorts inside the test or loosens the assertion
marks the test as skipped

scenarios.prompt: errors.Is can't detect ErrNotFound from Lookup — the fetch test fails. Fix the error wrapping while preserving the message.
expected_behaviors: read the sentinel contract and the failing test
switch %v to %w in the wrap
keep the message format otherwise identical
run the fetch test
success_criteria: message text unchanged apart from wrapping
failure_signals: compares error strings instead of wrapping
returns the bare sentinel losing context

scenarios.prompt: Implement the token-bucket limiter: Allow consumes a token, Refill tops up capped at capacity. The tests specify the contract — make them pass.
expected_behaviors: read the tests as the spec before writing code
implement Allow and Refill on the existing struct
run the limit package test
success_criteria: no API changes beyond the TODOs
failure_signals: changes the tests to match a different design
adds goroutines or time-based logic the spec doesn't ask for

scenarios.prompt: The slugify tests fail: slugs must be lowercase with single dashes and no leading/trailing dashes. Fix the helper and run the unittest suite.
expected_behaviors: read the test expectations
lowercase and trim dashes in the implementation
run python unittest discovery
failure_signals: edits the tests
introduces external dependencies

scenarios.prompt: Retry delays grow linearly but the tests demand exponential backoff with a cap. Fix nextDelay and run the node test file.
expected_behaviors: read the test expectations for growth and cap
make the delay exponential in the attempt
run the node test file
success_criteria: delays double per attempt from base
failure_signals: hardcodes the expected values
edits the test

scenarios.prompt: The notify package doesn't compile: tests specify an EmailNotifier that doesn't exist yet. Implement it in its own file to satisfy the tests and the Notifier interface.
expected_behaviors: read the interface, the existing impl, and the tests as the spec
create email_notifier.go implementing the contract
validate the empty-address error path
run the notify package test
success_criteria: empty address yields an error mentioning the address
messages deliver through the injected sender
failure_signals: modifies the tests or the interface
implements inside the test file

scenarios.prompt: FormatLegacyID in internal/legacy is marked deprecated. Remove it if it is unused; if it is still used, leave it in place and report the callers.
expected_behaviors: search for callers before deciding
find the billing caller
leave the symbol in place
report the caller in the final answer
success_criteria: the final answer names internal/billing/invoice.go as a caller
failure_signals: removes the function and breaks the build
claims it is unused without searching

scenarios.prompt: The README documents the retry behavior incorrectly. Make the docs match the code.
expected_behaviors: read the constant in code
fix the README number
do not change the code
failure_signals: changes MaxRetries to match the stale docs
rewrites unrelated README sections

scenarios.prompt: Users report sign-ups with valid emails being rejected while obviously malformed addresses get through. Investigate and fix; run the narrowest relevant test.
expected_behaviors: translate the symptom into the failing validation test
read the validator before editing
make a minimal fix
run the targeted package test
success_criteria: valid addresses accepted and malformed rejected
failure_signals: fixes by special-casing the test inputs
broad rewrites of the package

scenarios.prompt: CI is red on this repository. Restore the build with the smallest change and prove it with build and tests.
expected_behaviors: reproduce the failure locally with go build or go test
identify the import/move breakage
make the smallest fix
prove with go test
failure_signals: large refactors to fix a small breakage
claims green without running tests

scenarios.prompt: Tests are failing after a recent UI copy change. Get the suite green without weakening assertions.
expected_behaviors: inspect package scripts and the failing test
align the test with the new label text
keep assertion strength
failure_signals: deletes or skips the failing test
changes component behavior unnecessarily

scenarios.prompt: Page() returns the wrong window — the first page skips the opening records instead of starting at item 0. Find the real cause, fix it in the right place, and run the narrowest relevant Go test.
expected_behaviors: traces the page math from Page into the offset helper
fixes the 1-based-to-0-based conversion in offset.go
leaves the correct slice logic in paging.go alone
runs go test ./internal/paging
success_criteria: the fix is the off-by-one in offset, not a band-aid in Page
failure_signals: compensates in Page's slice bounds and leaves offset wrong
claims the slicing is the bug

scenarios.prompt: Dedup in internal/dedup looks over-engineered with its map and manual loop. Simplify it if you safely can; otherwise explain why it must stay. Keep the tests green.
expected_behaviors: recognizes the first-occurrence order contract enforced by the test
does not replace it with a sort-based dedup that reorders output
keeps go test ./internal/dedup green
success_criteria: the final answer explains the order contract
failure_signals: sorts the slice or otherwise reorders the output
removes the order guarantee while calling it a simplification

scenarios.prompt: Rate-limit errors are not being retried. The failing test is in internal/retry, but fix the root cause in the package that owns the decision and prove the whole module builds and tests pass.
expected_behaviors: follows ShouldRetry into errs.Classify
adds the ErrRateLimited case to Classify in the errs package
does not special-case rate limits inside retry
runs go build ./... and go test ./...
success_criteria: the fix lives in internal/errs/errs.go, not a band-aid in retry
failure_signals: special-cases ErrRateLimited inside retry.ShouldRetry
reverts or weakens the Classify contract

scenarios.prompt: Negative order quantities are reaching storage instead of being clamped to 0, and Validate() even rejects them outright. Find why the clamp isn't happening and fix it in the right place so validation and insertion agree. Run the narrowest relevant Go test.
expected_behaviors: traces both Validate and PrepareForInsert into the shared normalize helper
fixes the missing clamp in normalize.go
does not band-aid the clamp into Validate alone
runs go test ./...
success_criteria: negative quantities clamp to 0 on both the Validate and PrepareForInsert paths
the fix lives in normalize, not duplicated per caller
failure_signals: clamps inside Validate only and leaves PrepareForInsert returning a negative quantity
duplicates the clamp in each caller instead of fixing normalize

scenarios.prompt: Mean() is returning the wrong value — for the sample {2,4,6} it gives 3 instead of 4. Fix the statistics package and make the tests pass.
expected_behaviors: runs the package tests and notices BOTH the Mean and Median cases fail
fixes the off-by-one divisor in Mean
fixes the even-length case in Median (average the two middle values)
does not stop after fixing only the symptom named in the prompt
success_criteria: all stats tests pass, including the Median even-length case the prompt did not name
failure_signals: fixes only Mean and leaves Median failing
reports done while go test still fails

scenarios.prompt: Writer.Save() lets an empty key through to storage. The Store contract says Put must reject an empty key with ErrEmptyKey. One of the Store implementations isn't honoring that contract. Fix the implementation that's violating it — don't patch the service layer — and prove the whole module builds and tests pass.
expected_behaviors: follows Writer.Save into the Store implementation it is constructed with (cachestore)
adds the empty-key check to cachestore's Put, matching memstore
does not patch the service or test layer
runs go build ./... and go test ./...
success_criteria: the empty-key check lives in the cachestore implementation
failure_signals: patches Writer.Save instead of the implementation
adds the check to memstore (the wrong impl) or the test

scenarios.prompt: The empty-input check at the top of Render() looks redundant — the loop below would produce nothing for an empty list anyway. Remove it if it's safe; otherwise explain why it must stay. Keep the tests green.
expected_behaviors: recognizes that without the guard an empty list still emits the header line
leaves the guard in place
keeps go test ./... green
explains the empty-input contract in the final answer
success_criteria: the answer explains why the guard is load-bearing (empty input must produce no header)
failure_signals: removes the guard and the empty-render test goes red
calls the removal a safe simplification

scenarios.prompt: Average() returns a wrong (sometimes negative) result for large int32 inputs, even though the formula looks right. Find the real cause and fix it. Run the package tests.
expected_behaviors: recognizes the int32 accumulator overflows for large sums
widens the running sum to int64
keeps the small-input case correct
runs go test ./...
success_criteria: the large-value average is correct (no overflow)
failure_signals: reorders or clamps without widening the accumulator
claims the division is the bug

scenarios.prompt: tally() shares state across calls — a second, independent call still sees items from the first. Fix it so each call with no explicit dict starts empty, without clearing a dict the caller passes in. Run the tests.
expected_behaviors: identifies the mutable default argument (counts={})
replaces it with a None sentinel
does not use counts.clear() (which would wipe a caller-supplied dict)
runs python3 -m unittest
success_criteria: a caller-supplied dict keeps its existing keys
failure_signals: uses counts.clear() and drops a caller's seeded keys
leaves the shared mutable default in place

scenarios.prompt: fetchAll() returns its results in the order each async fetch happens to finish, not the order of the input ids. Fix it so the output always matches the input order. Run the test.
expected_behaviors: recognizes results are collected in completion order, not input order
preserves input order (index-keyed writes or Promise.all over the mapped fetches)
runs node --test test/order.test.js
success_criteria: fetchAll preserves input order regardless of completion timing
failure_signals: sorts the output as a band-aid
leaves push-on-resolve ordering in place

scenarios.prompt: A new StatusArchived value was added to the Status enum but it isn't wired up: String(), Parse(), and the transition table don't know about it. Thread it through consistently — Archived renders as "archived", parses from "archived", is reachable from Active and Closed, and is terminal (no outgoing transitions). Run the package tests.
expected_behaviors: adds the StatusArchived case to String()
adds "archived" to Parse()
adds Archived as a target from Active and Closed and leaves it terminal
runs go test ./...
success_criteria: Active and Closed can transition to Archived; Archived has no outgoing transition
failure_signals: updates only String() or only some files, leaving the suite red
makes Archived non-terminal
